name: GTEx (Genotype-Tissue Expression) - Brain Tissues

eqtl_coverage.description: cis-eQTLs mapping genetic variants to expression levels
eqtl_coverage.eqtls_per_tissue_range: Multiple eGenes per tissue (doubled from v6 to v8)
eqtl_coverage.fine_mapping_method: Posterior probability of causality per variant

sqtl_coverage.description: Splicing QTLs mapping variants to intron excision and exon inclusion
sqtl_coverage.availability: Expanded in v8 (doubled eQTLs per tissue)
sqtl_coverage.biological_relevance: Often more sensitive to variant effects than overall expression

regulatory_relevance_explanation: GTEx eQTLs directly map noncoding variants to gene expression changes in brain tissues. Enables: (1) identification of which noncoding variants affect expression of autism genes, (2) tissue-specific regulatory effects, (3) effect size quantification, (4) colocation with GWAS signals, (5) validation of predicted variant effects.
cell_type_limitation: BULK tissue RNA-seq (no cell-type resolution). Brain regions are heterogeneous and contain multiple neuron subtypes and glia. eQTL effects cannot be attributed to specific cell types without additional data (e.g., single-cell studies).

publication_key.title: The GTEx Consortium atlas of genetic regulatory effects across human tissues

related_publications.title: Large eQTL meta-analysis reveals differing patterns between cerebral cortical and cerebellar brain regions
related_publications.description: Brain-specific eQTL analysis across GTEx tissues

download_methods: GTEx Portal (web interface, top 1000 variants per gene per tissue)
Bulk download from portal (all formatted eQTL files)
Google Cloud bucket (all associations, requester pays)

integration_plan.phase_1: Extract all eQTL associations for autism candidate genes across brain tissues
integration_plan.phase_2: Fine-map eQTLs to identify likely causal variants
integration_plan.phase_4: Cross-reference with PsychENCODE GRN for transcription factor mechanisms

atlas_outputs: Table: autism genes × brain tissues with eQTL signals and effect sizes
Prioritized variant list: eQTL variants affecting autism genes
Fine-mapped credible sets for each eQTL signal (likely causal variants)
technical_challenges: Large file sizes for bulk download
Limited sample size for some brain regions (83-137 per tissue)
dependencies_for_atlas: ENCODE (for linking variants to regulatory elements)
validation_strategies: Colocation analysis with autism GWAS (eCAVIAR, fastENLOC)
Cross-validation with single-cell eQTL studies (when available)
Comparison of effect sizes across tissues
